name: Unreal MCP
what_it_does: Unreal MCP lets AI assistant clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf) control Unreal Engine 5 through natural language via MCP, enabling actor creation, Blueprint scripting, viewport control, and level design automation through a C++ TCP plugin and Python MCP server bridge.
use_cases: Using Claude or Cursor to create and position actors in a UE5 level through conversational commands
Automating Blueprint class creation, node graph wiring, and variable setup from natural language descriptions
Controlling the UE5 editor viewport and camera focus programmatically from an AI agent
not_for: Production pipelines requiring stable, non-breaking APIs (project is explicitly Experimental)
Teams needing runtime in-game AI integration (editor-only tool)
best_when: You are a UE5 developer who wants to use Claude Desktop or Cursor to accelerate level design and Blueprint creation through conversational AI, and you are comfortable with experimental tooling.
avoid_when: You need production-stable tooling or are on UE5 versions older than 5.5.

auth.notes: No authentication. TCP server on port 55557 runs locally.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Experimental status suggests error handling may be incomplete.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Scene manipulation is stateful; idempotency not documented.
known_agent_gotchas: Explicitly marked Experimental — breaking changes expected
Three-component architecture (C++ plugin + Python server + sample project) adds setup complexity
TCP server on fixed port 55557 — port conflicts possible

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
